segments.text: Sing aloud to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob.
Start the song! Play the tambourine, sweet-sounding lyre, and harp.
Blow the trumpet at the new moon, and at full moon, to begin our festivals,
for this is a rule for Israel, a regulation of the God of Jacob.
God made this statute for Joseph when he opposed the land of Egypt. I heard a voice I didn't know, saying:
I took the load from your shoulders; I freed your hands from carrying heavy baskets.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In your suffering you cried out to me, and I saved you. I answered you from the thundercloud. I tested you at the waters of Meribah.  Selah.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: My people, listen to my warnings! Israel, please listen to me!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: There must not be a strange god among you; you must never bow down to foreign gods and worship them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But my people didn't listen to me. Israel didn't want anything to do with me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So I sent them away to follow their stubborn thinking, living as they chose.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If only my people would listen to me; if only Israel would follow my ways!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It wouldn't take me long to conquer their enemies, to strike down their foes.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Those who hate the Lord would cringe before him, forever doomed.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But I would feed you the best wheat and satisfy you with honey from the rock.
segments.speaker: God
